svg.title: GTM agent full multi-layer architecture
svg.desc: Five-layer system: user input flows to LangGraph orchestrator which spawns four research sub-agents (Reddit, Twitter, web search, competitor) all sharing a Redis context store, then three reasoning agents (segmentation, positioning, channel strategy) synthesize findings, and a writer agent produces the final GTM report PDF. Infrastructure: FastAPI, Celery plus Redis, Supabase, LangSmith, Vercel.

g.onclick: sendPrompt('What does the user input form look like for the GTM agent? What fields does it need?')
sendPrompt('How does the LangGraph orchestrator decompose a product description into parallel research tasks?')
sendPrompt('What specific data does the Reddit sub-agent extract — pain points, sentiment, feature requests?')
sendPrompt('How does the Twitter sub-agent extract real-time sentiment and trending discussions about a market?')
sendPrompt('How does the web search sub-agent use Tavily and Jina together for semantic research and extraction?')
sendPrompt('What competitor data does the competitor sub-agent collect, and how does it structure its findings?')
sendPrompt('Explain exactly how the shared Redis context store enables peer-to-peer communication between research and reasoning agents.')
sendPrompt('How does the segmentation agent identify ideal customer profiles and personas from the research data?')
sendPrompt('How does the positioning agent synthesize research into product positioning and messaging frameworks?')
sendPrompt('How does the channel strategy agent rank launch channels and design A/B messaging experiments?')
sendPrompt('How does the writer agent structure the final GTM document — what sections and format does it produce?')
sendPrompt('What does the final GTM strategy report contain? Walk me through each section of the output document.')
sendPrompt('Give me a summary of the full tech stack for this GTM agent project.')
g.text: FastAPI · Celery + Redis · Supabase · LangSmith · Vercel
